verses.text: L'attente exprimée dans ces versets était clairement formulée dans le Chema, la grande déclaration de foi et d'engagement de l'alliance d'Israël (cf. **6.4** ). L'obéissance à l'alliance était une condition préalable nécessaire pour qu'Israël reçoive le pardon de Dieu et retourne dans son pays.

L'attente exprimée dans ces versets était clairement formulée dans le Chema, la grande déclaration de foi et d'engagement de l'alliance d'Israël (cf. **6.4** ). L'obéissance à l'alliance était une condition préalable nécessaire pour qu'Israël reçoive le pardon de Dieu et retourne dans son pays.

30:4 If any of thine be driven out unto the outmost parts of heaven,.... As many of them are in this remote island of ours, Great Britain, reckoned formerly the uttermost part of the earth, as Thule, supposed to be Schetland, an isle belonging to Scotland, is said to be (m); See Gill on Deu 28:49; and as some of them are thought to be in America, which Manasseh Ben Israel (n) had a firm belief of: from thence will the Lord thy God gather thee, and from thence will he fetch thee;

30:5 And the Lord thy God will bring thee into the land which thy fathers possessed, and thou shalt possess it,.... That the Jews upon their conversion in the latter day will return to the land of Judea again, and possess it, is the sense of many passages of Scripture; among others, see Jer 30:18; the above Targum is;"the Word of the Lord will bring thee, &c." and he will do thee good; both in things temporal and spiritual; see Ezek 34:24, and multiply thee above thy fathers;
Cette œuvre de Dieu, initiée par sa grâce, aboutira à la régénération d'Israël et identifiera la nation comme le véritable peuple du Seigneur (voir **10.16 ** ; **Jr 4:4 ** ; **Rm 4.1-12** ).

30:7 And the Lord thy God will put all these curses upon thine enemies,.... Recorded in Deu 28:16; that is, the Word of the Lord, as the Targum of Jonathan; for he being now sought unto, and embraced, will be their King and their Saviour, and revenge their enemies: and on them that hate thee, which persecuted thee: the Turks and Papists, the former having taken possession of their land, and the latter being violent persecutors of them in all their countries.

30:8 And thou shall return, and obey the voice of the Lord,.... In the Gospel; obey the form of doctrine delivered to them, embrace the truth of the Gospel cordially, and yield a cheerful subjection to the ordinances of it; or receive the Word of the Lord, as the Targum of Jonathan, Christ, the essential Word, by faith, as the Saviour and Redeemer, Mediator, Prophet, Priest, and King: and do all his commandments which I command thee this day; the commandment nigh unto them, in their mouths and hearts, even the word of faith, the ministers of Christ preach, Deu 30:11;

30:9 And the Lord thy God will make thee plenteous in every work of thine hand,.... In every manufacture, trade, or business of life in which they should be employed; the meaning is, that the Lord will greatly bless them in all that they shall set their hands to in a lawful way; so that they shall abound in good things, and have enough and to spare, a redundancy of the good things of life, great plenty of them: in the fruit of thy body;

30:10 If thou shalt hearken unto the voice of the Lord thy God,.... Or rather, "for thou shalt hearken" (p); seeing it is before promised that the Lord would circumcise their hearts to love him, and they should obey his voice, Deu 30:6. The Targum is,"receive the Word of the Lord:" to keep his commandments and his statutes, which are written in this book of the law; particularly in this chapter, and all that are of a moral nature: and if thou return unto the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul;
Ce commandement renvoie à tout le texte de l'alliance, tout le Deutéronome (voir **4.2 ** ; **5.29 ** ; **7.9** ).
La révélation de Dieu n'est pas connue seulement de lui mais est également accessible aux êtres humains (voir **Rm 10.6-8** ).
Les desseins de Dieu ne sont pas confiés à des nations lointaines mais sont à la disposition de son peuple (voir **Rm 10.6-8** ).
Le message du salut est objectivement proche (sur les lèvres) et subjectivement proche (dans le cœur). Il peut être enseigné et appris (voir **6.6-7** ).

30:15 DEATH AND LIFE ARE SET BEFORE THE ISRAELITES. (Deu 30:15-20) See, I have set before thee this day life and good, and death and evil—the alternative of a good and happy, or a disobedient and miserable life. Love of God and compliance with His will are the only ways of securing the blessings and avoiding the evils described. The choice was left to them, and in urging upon them the inducements to a wise choice, Moses warmed as he proceeded into a tone of solemn and impressive earnestness similar to that of Paul to the elders of Ephesus (Acts 20:26-27).
Ceci paraphrase le Chema, l'affirmation centrale de l'alliance d'Israël (voir **6.5** ). Aimer Dieu signifie lui obéir en tout point, ce qui entraîne une bénédiction divine.

30:17 But if thine heart turn away,.... From the true God, and the right worship of him, and from his commands, statutes, and judgments: so that thou wilt not hear; the voice of the Lord and obey it, or hearken to his laws, and do them: but shalt be drawn away; by an evil heart, and by the ill examples of others: and worship other gods and serve them; the gods of the Gentiles, the idols of the people, besides the one living and true God, the God of Israel.

30:18 I denounce unto, you this day that ye shall surely perish,.... By one judgment or another; this he most solemnly averred, and it might be depended upon that it would certainly be their case; see Deu 4:26, and that ye shall not prolong your days upon the land, whither thou passest over Jordan to go to possess it; but be cut short by death, of one kind or another, by sword, or famine, or pestilence, or be carried into captivity;

Cette structure grammaticale suggère une équivalence entre la vie et la bénédiction et entre la mort et les malédictions. L'essence d'une bénédiction est la vie, et l'essence d'une malédiction est la mort.
Dans son sens le plus significatif, la vie consiste en un engagement absolu envers le Seigneur.
